reviews.review_msg: This class was Kevin at his best. It was a special topics course in Financial Economics, where Kevin had plenty of anecdotes that helped the lectures flow. This class is relatively easy in everything except exams and quizzes. They are hell. Definitely go to office hours/review sessions to study for them. Great Prof but needs to chill on exams
Don't take this class with him. Boring lectures and tough grader.
Great prof! It was his first time teaching this class and he was great! was will to give feedback on your writing and wants you to improve! Would 10/10 recommend taking 220W with him.
种锅仁布片种锅仁。这老湿给A忒别朵。给份很松

The lectures are boring, and he has many random tests without notice to grade participation. I learned a lot from his lectures and tutorials, but in the tests, the questions are very complicated, not only for me but also for most of the students. He is a strict grader.

I WENT TO ALL THE LECTURES DID MY QUIZZES AND EVERYTHING BUT TELL ME WHY THE EXAM WAS NOTHING LIKE WHAT WE PRACTICED??? LIKE HE SAYS U ONLY NEED TUTORIAL BUT LIKE ITS NOT EVEN THE SAME AND THE GRADING IS BAD TOO AFTER ALL THAT STUDYING I SOMEHOW BARELY PASSES??? WHAT IS THIS PROF DONT TAKE PLS SAVE UR GPA- SO HARD EXAMS!!!!

The class was quite difficult but he is a very generous marker and you will get a lot of half points for just showing an attempt at the question. My only real gripe is that at times it was unclear what exactly would be tested on the midterm and the final. Overall, I don't think Professor Laughren is as bad as his current rating suggests.

He rushed through the material in class and wasn't helpful during office hours. He looked visibly annoyed or angry when I asked him to go over concepts again. Made me feel like it was my problem I was struggling.

Amazing class! The grading criteria were very clear. The lectures were amazing, and he clearly understands the material and conveys it well. He genuinely seems to enjoy teaching, and it shows in his work. The class had super active discussion boards. He's very approachable and is one of the best econ professors I've had throughout my degree!
It was fun and useful class. Visiting office hour was very helpful.

He's a nice guy while teaching in class, but not when grading assignments. Feedback was very nit picky, doesn't say much or even anything about what was done right, but lots of comments on what was wrong. Avoid him if you can, although he's assigned most teaching duties in the department being a lecturer.
I liked taking the class with him. He is super nice and very approachable. Would recommend taking classes with him.
The course was unnecessarily complicated and the exams were too hard. Not recommended. I also found the professor annoying and weird. He's nice but definitely weird.

His lectures are great, and while there were some issues with the assignment expectations, there was 
a great deal of grading leniency and he improved the criteria as the course went along. The other reviews for this course are little confusing as the class average was an A-.

Unfortunately, not helpful at all. His responses to questions were often vague or dismissive. Office hours didn't offer much support, and the lectures felt rushed or unclear. It seemed like we were expected to already understand the material. I don't recommend.

Kevin is a good performer, gregarious and could give interesting lectures. But he's not a very helpful professor. Expectations weren't clear and grading was ridiculous and inconsistent. He'll look like he's doing a good job, but in reality he's ineffective.
Just do his classes and you will know the truth. Also one of the worst graders of all time. Do not recommend at all. 0/10

Most certainly has bias towards students. Has his favourites who he will mark good regardless, others he'll just end up giving what he thinks you deserve rather than the actual assignment. If you talk to him about it, he will just dismiss you.

Don't take his class(es?) unless you're prepared to do everything with very little help. The entire experience was so stressful that by the end I started to question my own competence. I mentioned this to Kevin and felt completely dismissed. The grading is harsh for sure, and you're expected to know things that are not even listed as prerequisites.

One of the best professors I've had during my four years at university. I believe those who gave low ratings likely didn't put in the effort themselves. If you're not willing to work hard, you shouldn't expect to pass. He is definitely a fair grader!
Seems like a nice guy but not helpful at all and has some issues. Did not enjoy the course experience.
Not responsive and does not help as much. Professor does not provide proper feedback or even explain his own materials. Harsh grader too.

I had him for ECON342 in his first term ever at SFU (Fall 2024) and could say that he's one of the best Econ instructors I've ever had during my major. His teaching approach was clear, the exam questions may be a little tricky compared to his weekly assignments (done in tutorial) but if you study hard and understand the concepts, you'll do well.

Literally took another ECON class because he was teaching it, If that doesnt say how good of a prof he is, I dont know what. Provides clear feedback, takes time to learn everyone's name, and accessible after class. Would definitely recommend him to anyone that would take the course.
Kevin Laughren is one of the best professors in the economics department. He has a clear approach to the course and encourages active participation in his classes. Definitely recommended!
